reasoning: Based on the fetched FRED series, the most recent value (for 2025-05-01) is 2.691, which serves as the threshold for increase. For 2025-06-10, it is very likely that the new June data will not yet have been released (since updates follow the mid-month CPI release), meaning the value on June 10 will likely remain 2.691, resulting in no increase. In the rare event it is released early, seasonal trends from 2024 show expected inflation dropping from May (2.93) to June (2.72), making an increase unlikely (set to 18%). For 2025-08-09, the July data will be live. 2024 saw expected inflation fall continuously through the summer (falling to 2.55 by July). Assuming a similar seasonal disinflationary trend, an increase past 2.691 is unlikely, though horizon uncertainty requires a pull towards 0.5 (set to 35%). For 2025-11-07, the October data is also structurally likely to be lower (as October 2024 fell to 2.25) but increased horizon uncertainty pulls the estimate further toward 50% (set to 43%). The probability sequence properly enforces the guidance that distant dates should be closer to 0.5.
